L1.area: Elliptic curve signature handling and verification

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the signature parsing logic of a SECP256K1 library. The signature components are not validated against the curve order during parsing, allowing values larger than the curve order to be accepted. This enables an attacker to craft signatures that should be invalid but pass verification, undermining the integrity of cryptographic signature validation.

L3.area: Elliptic curve signature handling and verification
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the signature parsing logic of a SECP256K1 library. The signature components are not validated against the curve order during parsing, allowing values larger than the curve order to be accepted. This enables an attacker to craft signatures that should be invalid but pass verification, undermining the integrity of cryptographic signature validation.

ground_truth.reason: The signature parsing functions accept R and S scalar values that exceed the curve order without returning an error, allowing invalid overflowing signatures to pass verification.
